title: Guitar Basics: Parts & Strings
description: Learn the basic parts of a guitar and understand the strings

questions.question: How many strings does a standard acoustic guitar have?
questions.explanation: A standard acoustic guitar has 6 strings tuned to E-A-D-G-B-E from lowest to highest.

questions.question: What is the head of the guitar?
options: The part at the end of the neck with tuning pegs
questions.correctAnswer: The part at the end of the neck with tuning pegs
questions.explanation: The headstock (or head) is located at the end of the guitar neck and holds the tuning pegs.

questions.question: True or False: The fretboard is where you press your fingers to change the notes.
questions.explanation: The fretboard contains metal strips called frets that divide the neck into sections. Pressing strings against different frets changes the pitch.

questions.question: Which of these is NOT a part of a guitar?
questions.explanation: A piston is not a part of a guitar. Soundhole, pickguard, and bridge are all essential parts.

questions.question: What does the bridge do on a guitar?
questions.explanation: The bridge anchors the strings on the body of the guitar and transfers string vibrations to the soundboard.

title: Guitar Basics: Open Chords
description: Master the fundamental open chords every guitarist should know

questions.question: Which of these is an open chord?
questions.explanation: C Major is a common open chord using fingers on the 1st and 2nd frets. F#, B, and A# are typically played as barre chords.

questions.question: True or False: An open chord means at least one string is played open without fretting.
questions.explanation: An open chord includes at least one open string (not fretted) in the chord shape.

questions.question: The G Major chord is formed primarily on which frets?
questions.explanation: G Major uses the 1st and 3rd frets with one open string (the high E string).

title: Piano Keys: Notes & Layout
description: Understand the piano keyboard layout and note names

questions.question: How many keys are on a standard piano?
questions.explanation: A standard piano has 88 keys spanning from A0 to C8.

questions.question: Which key color is more common on a piano?
questions.explanation: There are 52 white keys and 36 black keys on a standard 88-key piano.

questions.question: True or False: Middle C is in the middle of the piano keyboard.
questions.explanation: Middle C (C4) is approximately in the center of an 88-key piano keyboard.

questions.question: How many black keys are in each octave?
questions.explanation: Each octave contains 12 keys: 7 white keys and 5 black keys.

title: Drums: Basic Components
description: Learn the parts of a drum kit and their names

questions.question: What is the largest drum in a basic drum kit called?
questions.explanation: The bass drum (or kick drum) is the largest drum, played with a foot pedal.

questions.question: True or False: Cymbals are made of metal.
questions.explanation: Cymbals are made of brass or bronze alloys and produce metallic sounds.

questions.question: Which drum typically has wires underneath it?
questions.explanation: The snare drum has metal wires (called snares) on the bottom that vibrate to create its characteristic sound.

title: Violin: Structure & Parts
description: Understand the basic structure of a violin

questions.question: How many strings does a violin have?
questions.explanation: A standard violin has 4 strings tuned to G-D-A-E from lowest to highest.

questions.question: What is the bow used for in violin playing?
options: Producing sound by rubbing the strings
questions.correctAnswer: Producing sound by rubbing the strings
questions.explanation: The bow contains horsehair that rubs against the strings to create vibrations and sound.

questions.question: True or False: The chin rest is part of the violin's body.
questions.explanation: The chin rest is mounted on the lower bout of the violin, helping the player support and stabilize the instrument.

title: Barre Chords & Techniques
description: Build your skills with barre chords and intermediate techniques

questions.question: What is a barre chord?
options: A chord played on open strings only
A chord where one finger presses multiple strings across different frets
A chord that uses only black strings
questions.correctAnswer: A chord where one finger presses multiple strings across different frets
questions.explanation: A barre chord uses one finger (usually the index) pressed across multiple strings on the same fret to change pitch.

questions.question: Which finger is most commonly used for barre chords?
questions.explanation: The index finger is typically used for barre chords because it's the longest and strongest.

questions.question: True or False: Palm muting produces a softer, more controlled sound.
questions.explanation: Palm muting involves resting the palm of your picking hand on the strings to dampen the sound and create a muted effect.

title: Scales & Arpeggios
description: Master scales and arpeggios for fluent playing

questions.question: How many notes are in a major scale?
questions.explanation: A major scale consists of 7 notes (plus the octave, which repeats the first note).

questions.question: What is an arpeggio?
options: A chord played one note at a time
questions.correctAnswer: A chord played one note at a time
questions.explanation: An arpeggio breaks up a chord and plays its notes sequentially rather than simultaneously.
